skill.name: requesting-code-review
skill.description: Creates a structured code review request for a pull request or merge request, including scope, git range, risk areas, test expectations, and acceptance criteria.
skill.summary: Prepare consistent code review briefs before merging changes.
skill.author: CodingCossack

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged shell command examples, weak cryptography keywords, and system reconnaissance keywords. Manual review found documented git commands, SHA placeholder references, and ordinary review checklist text. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, credential access, network access, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: Documented Git Commands Require Trusted Inputs
low_findings.description: The skill shows git command examples for finding commit ranges and reviewing diffs. These are legitimate for a code review skill, but users should insert trusted commit hashes or validated branch references before running commands in a shell.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are clearly git review examples, not hidden execution logic. Risk remains low because placeholder values could be unsafe if a user substitutes untrusted shell input.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis matched SHA terminology, but the files discuss git commit SHAs and placeholders. No hashing implementation, password storage, signature verification, or cryptographic decision was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed context contains git SHA placeholders and ordinary review language only. There is no code path that uses a weak cryptographic algorithm.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis matched generic words in review instructions and example output. The skill does not gather host, network, user, environment, or process information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no reconnaissance commands or data collection behavior. The cited lines are checklist or example text for code review output.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill contains shell snippets for git merge-base, git rev-parse, git log, grep, awk, and git diff. These are expected for reviewing code ranges and should be executed only in trusted repositories.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell commands are explicit and relevant to the skill purpose. They are not malicious, but command execution is a real operational risk factor.

content.value_statement: Code review requests often miss context, risk areas, or test expectations. This skill helps Claude, Codex, or Claude Code prepare a clear review brief before merge.
actual_capabilities: Guides users to define a git base and head range for review.
Provides a reusable code reviewer prompt template.
Organizes review context, implementation details, and requirements.
Requests severity-based findings with file and line references.
Produces a merge readiness assessment with reasoning.
Encourages action on critical and important review feedback.
limitations: Does not run tests or verify repository state by itself.
Requires accurate git SHAs and implementation context from the user.
Does not replace human judgment for high-risk production changes.
May miss issues if the provided diff range is incomplete.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Pull Request Review
use_cases.description: Create a complete review request with scope, git range, requirements, and expected output.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Review Handoffs
use_cases.description: Use the same brief format for each merge request so reviewers get consistent context.

use_cases.title: Check Production Readiness
use_cases.description: Ask an AI reviewer to focus on severity, test coverage, architecture, and release risk.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Review Request
prompt_templates.scenario: Use after finishing a small pull request.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the requesting-code-review skill to prepare a review brief for my latest change. Ask me for the implementation summary, requirements, base SHA, and head SHA.

prompt_templates.title: Review Against Requirements
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when the work must match a plan or ticket.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a code review request for this change. Compare the implementation against the requirements I provide, and highlight missing behavior, test gaps, and merge blockers.

prompt_templates.title: Production Readiness Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use before merging a risky or customer-facing change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a production readiness review request. Include architecture concerns, migration risks, backward compatibility, security concerns, test coverage, and a clear ready-to-merge verdict.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Commit Range Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use for a feature branch with several commits.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the code reviewer template for a full branch review from my base SHA to head SHA. Focus on cross-file behavior, hidden regressions, edge cases, and release risk.

output_examples.input: A developer finished a feature branch and needs a review request before opening a pull request.
output_examples.output: A structured review brief with implementation summary, plan reference, base and head commits, review checklist, and expected severity categories.

output_examples.input: An engineering lead wants consistent review feedback across several tasks in a delivery plan.
output: A repeatable prompt for the reviewer.
A checklist covering code quality, architecture, tests, requirements, and production readiness.
A verdict format that separates critical, important, and minor issues.

output_examples.input: A release manager wants to know whether a change is ready to merge.
output_examples.output: A review request that asks for strengths, blocking issues, recommendations, and a concise merge readiness assessment.

best_practices: Use exact commit hashes for the base and head range.
Include the original requirements or plan with the review request.
Fix critical and important findings before continuing to the next task.
anti_patterns: Requesting review without a clear diff range.
Treating vague positive feedback as a completed review.
Ignoring valid high-severity findings because the change seems small.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates a structured prompt for requesting a code review of a pull request, merge request, or commit range.

faq.question: Does it review the code directly?
faq.answer: It provides the review request template. The actual review is performed by Claude, Codex, Claude Code, or another reviewer.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide what changed, the requirements, the base commit, the head commit, and any known risk areas.

faq.question: Can it be used with any repository?
faq.answer: Yes, if the repository uses git and you can identify the correct commit range for review.

faq.question: Is it safe to run?
faq.answer: The skill contains git command examples. Run them only in trusted repositories and use trusted commit references.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
